scope_notes: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh, hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py, and hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context_rules.py are shell/Python files — outside prompt verbosity inspection scope. Only commands/commit.md (Markdown command documentation) was analyzed.

commands_commit_md.verdict: No threshold-qualifying verbosity violations. Pre-existing ## Usage at commands/commit.md:12-24 is concise: 13 lines out of 293 total = 4.4%; below both count and percentage thresholds; introduced_in_diff=false. All other critical-pattern headers (Philosophy, Overview, Quality Standards, Safety Features, Helper Scripts) are absent.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 2 actionable issues and 1 observation-only. Issue 1: draft rationale overstated 'no critical verbose patterns matched' — commands/commit.md:12 has a pre-existing ## Usage header which appears in the critical-pattern list; it is 13 lines / 4.4% and below all thresholds, but should be acknowledged. Issue 2: threshold rationale cited only the 50-line threshold; the spec requires both line-count and percentage thresholds to be referenced. Observation: zero-finding conclusion is correct.

findings.issue: Draft rationale overstated 'no critical verbose patterns matched.' commands/commit.md:12 has a pre-existing ## Usage header listed in agents/prompt-inspector.md critical-pattern list. It is concise and below all thresholds, but the rationale should acknowledge the pattern header exists.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with: 'No threshold-qualifying verbosity violations. Pre-existing ## Usage at commands/commit.md:12-24 is concise: 13 lines / 293 total = 4.4%, below reporting thresholds; introduced_in_diff=false. Other critical-pattern headers are absent.'
findings.rationale: below_threshold_notes.commands_commit_md now explicitly acknowledges the ## Usage pattern header and reports it as below both line-count and percentage thresholds with introduced_in_diff=false.

findings.issue: Threshold rationale cited only the 50-line threshold; prompt-inspector severity uses line count AND percentage thresholds.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace 'below 50-line warning threshold' with: 'below both line-count and percentage thresholds: 13 verbose-pattern lines out of 293 total = 4.4%; no finding.'
findings.rationale: below_threshold_notes.commands_commit_md now references both the line-count threshold (13 < 50 lines) and the percentage threshold (4.4% < 15%).

findings.issue: The zero-finding conclusion is otherwise correct for this request's scoped changed files. The only in-scope Markdown file is commands/commit.md, and the diff only renames the procedural heading at line 221.
findings.rationale: Confirms zero findings is the correct verdict.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Acknowledged pre-existing ## Usage pattern header at commands/commit.md:12 in below_threshold_notes, with explicit reference to both line-count (13 < 50) and percentage (4.4% < 15%) thresholds. introduced_in_diff=false tag applied per Mechanism (ii) tagging contract.
